quranicRelevance: Cities/towns (Qaryah / Qura), mother of cities (Umm al-Qura - Mecca), and hosting guests (Qira).
figurativeOrRareUsages: أُمَّ القُرَى: Mecca (the mother of cities, representing the spiritual center).
وَاسْأَلِ القَرْيَةَ (12:82): Ask the town (meaning ask the people of the town).
discardedNoise: Hosting guests and serving food (قرى الضيف).
summaryEn: The root denotes habitation and urban centers, primarily referring to cities (Qura) and the people inhabiting them.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على العمران والمدن، مشيراً بشكل رئيسي إلى القرى والمدن وسكانها في القصص القرآني.
rawText: اِفْتَرَى 8 [meaning تَتَبَّعَ: see a verse of Aboo Dhu-eyb, voce خَافَةٌ, in art. خوف.] It also means He investigated a country or countries. (S, * K, * TA, all in art. قرى; and TA in art. قرو.) He made much and diligent search. (KL.) See also 10 in art. قرأ. قَارٍ for قَارِئٌ, q. v. قَارِيَهٌ A certain bird. See خُضَارِىٌّ and تَنَوُّطٌ. قَرَى الضَّيْفَ 1 is doubly trans.: see a verse cited voce قُوهٌ. قَرِىٌّ A place where water runs, (T, S,) to, (T,) or in, or into, (S,) meadows, (T,) or a meadow. (S.) See شَيْخٌ (last sentence). === Pl. أَقْرِيَةٌ: see قَرْءٌ. قَرِيَّةٌ [vulg. قَرْيَة The yard of a ship;] a squared piece of wood upon the head of the mast of a ship. (Az, TA in art. رنح.) قَرْيَةٌ A town, or village; (Msb, TA;) a small بَلَد, smaller than a مَدِينَة: (MF, voce تِرْمِذ:) not well applied to a مَدِينَة unless qualified by an epithet denoting greatness. (TA in art. مَدِينَة.) See Bd, ii. 261. قِرًى Entertainment for a guest; that with which a guest is entertained. (S.) === Water collected in a trough, or tank, for the drinking of beasts: see بَيُّوتٌ: thus explained in the M in art. بيت. مَقْرًى : see 2 in art. حجل. مَقْرُوٌّ and مَقْرِىٌّ for مَقْرُوْءٌ: see art. قرأ. نَاقَةٌ فِى قِرْوَتِهَا : see 4 in art. قرأ. نَاقَةٌ قَرْوَاءُ A long-backed she-camel. (IB, in TA, voce هِرْجَابٌ.)
